messages: The room feels a little easier to read, and the cashier marks the safer exits.

message: You buy a quick drink. Luck lifts, but the room gets easier to read wrong.

messages: The drink warms your nerve and loosens your timing.

message: You make the family call and hear the strings before the cash.

messages: The line clicks dead; your brother-in-law will answer the ask now.

message: The sax player nods over the rim and lets the solo curl around your luck.

messages: The sax player remembers the round.

message: The cellist smiles into a low walking line and keeps time with your nerve.
The drummer taps the glass twice, then rolls the groove forward.
You feed the band jar and let chance carry the round.

messages: The band notices the tip jar.

message: You stay through the changes and let the room swing around you.

messages: The band notices you stayed for the music.

message: The glasses make people rethink what they saw.

messages: Local heat drops away under the drummer's name.

message: The lounge sends over champagne and the room softens at the edges.

messages: Champagne buys a little grace, but not a steady hand.

message: The lights hit the stage and half the room forgets to watch you.

messages: The show gives your heat somewhere else to go.

message: Cold river air clears your head while the dock stays out of reach.

messages: The river air cools attention without ending the ride.

message: The surf gives the heat time to fade.

messages: The surf cools the room's attention.
